subject: Science
chapter: Adolescence: Stage of Growth and Change

activities_projects.title: Predicting Final Height
activities_projects.objective: Use mathematical formulas to estimate adult height
steps: Measure your current height in cm
Find the percentage of full height for your age from the textbook chart
Calculate: (Current height / % of full height at this age) x 100
Compare results between different age groups in your class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies mathematical calculation to biological growth data
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct use of the formula and data interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Endocrine Gland Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Locate and identify the functions of the endocrine system
steps: Draw or use an outline of the human body
Mark the exact positions of Pituitary, Thyroid, Adrenal, and Pancreas
Color-code glands that are gender-specific (Testes and Ovaries)
Write the primary function of each gland next to the label
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the spatial arrangement and roles of the endocrine system
activities_projects.evaluation: Anatomical accuracy and correct functional labeling (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hormonal Feedback Loop Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand how hormones regulate body changes
steps: Create a flow diagram showing the Pituitary gland as the 'Master Gland'
Draw arrows to show how it signals the Testes or Ovaries
Describe how Testosterone and Estrogen lead to secondary sexual characters
Illustrate the 'stop/start' feedback mechanism
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the chemical signaling path in the human body
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the feedback loop logic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Menstrual Cycle Calendar Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the biological timeline of the female reproductive cycle
steps: Mark a hypothetical 28-day cycle on the calendar
Identify and label the menstruation phase, the ovulation phase, and the thickening of the uterine wall
Explain what happens to the egg if fertilization does not occur
Discuss the importance of hygiene during this period
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Gains scientific understanding of the menstrual cycle phases
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of phase timing and biological descriptions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Nutritional Deficiency Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of diet on adolescent development
steps: Research common deficiencies in teenagers (Iron/Anemia, Calcium)
Write a report on why adolescent girls need more iron-rich food
List 5 locally available foods that prevent these deficiencies
Explain the role of Iodine in the functioning of the Thyroid gland
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates nutritional science to hormonal and physical health
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research and practical food recommendations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Chemistry of Sweat and Sebaceous Glands
activities_projects.objective: Investigate why acne and body odor increase during puberty
steps: Observe the skin pores on the nose and forehead area
Research the difference between sweat glands and sebaceous (oil) glands
Explain the chemical reason why bacteria thrive on oily skin during adolescence
Draft a 'Skin Care Routine' based on these scientific facts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects glandular activity to common adolescent skin changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific accuracy in explaining gland secretions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Determination of Sex: Genetic Probabilities
activities_projects.objective: Learn how the sex of a baby is determined genetically
materials: Two colors of beads (X and Y)
steps: Draw a cross-diagram (Punnett square) for XY (Male) and XX (Female)
Show the possible combinations of chromosomes in the zygote
Calculate the percentage chance of a male or female child
Explain why the father's sperm determines the sex of the child
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands chromosomal inheritance and sex determination
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness of the genetic cross-diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Adolescent Brain: Logic vs. Emotion
activities_projects.objective: Explore the mental development stage of adolescence
steps: Research the development of the 'Prefrontal Cortex' (logic) vs. the 'Amygdala' (emotions) in teens
Create an infographic showing why adolescents may take more risks
Suggest three 'Brain Exercises' to improve focus and decision making
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops self-awareness regarding cognitive and emotional shifts
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the infographic and scientific reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Metamorphosis vs. Human Puberty
activities_projects.objective: Compare hormonal changes across different species
steps: Study the role of Thyroxine in frogs (tadpole to frog)
Study the role of Ecdysone in insects
Compare these 'metamorphosis' hormones with human 'growth' hormones
Note the similarities in how hormones trigger life-stage transitions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Broadens the understanding of hormones as universal biological triggers
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative analysis and identification of similarities (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Community Awareness Survey
activities_projects.objective: Identify social myths and educate others
steps: Prepare 5 questions about puberty and reproductive health
Survey 10 people (anonymously) to check for common myths
Analyze the data: Which myth is most common?
Prepare a one-page 'Fact Sheet' to address the findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops data analysis and community education skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Data presentation and effectiveness of the Fact Sheet (10 marks)
